methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $400M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Solugen, a Series D-stage climate and energy technology company that has raised $400M+, is evaluated against a peer set of four same-sector comparables at Series C and D stages: Redwood Materials ($6.0B), KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M). This reflects a broader landscape of 9 private frontier-tech companies in the energy transition space plus 1 public comparable.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $400M total capital raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implies $600M. Peer-median analysis across the four Climate & Energy comparables yields a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range spanning $700M to $6.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and commercialization stage within the peer cohort.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $510M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a midpoint of approximately $1.1B. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound, while peer comparables support the higher end. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the peer set given variation in business model, path to revenue, and capital efficiency across climate tech verticals, as well as the representativeness of $400M raised as a valuation proxy absent explicit fundraising terms.
